content: The 2024-2025 SOTA push to optimize active learning agent memory can be reframed as a hierarchical curiosity pruning problem, where the agent’s own sense of novelty acts as a dynamic filter that prioritizes which long-term memory traces to retain, rather than relying on static importance-scoring retention rules. This creates a self-reinforcing loop where more curious exploration produces more targeted, useful memory that in turn enables more efficient active learning.
